senses.gloss: spiny-tailed lizard, mastigure (any lizard of the genus Uromastyx)

examples.text:
876, The Letter of Yaʿqūb al-Layṯ to Al-Muʿtamid, written by the poet Ibrāhīm ibn Mamšāḏ
فعودوا الى ارضكم بالحجاز
لاكل الضباب ورعي الغنم
So return to your [own] land in Hejaz [Arabia], to eat lizards and to graze [your] sheep

senses.gloss: to cleave to, to hold to firmly
to have a gentle or scanty flow, to stream, to discharge sluggishly
to be affected by the disease termed ضَبّ (ḍabb)
a disease in the lips, in consequence of which blood flows from it
tumor in the extremity of the foot of a camel
chapping or cracking in the armpit of a child or camel
spadix of a palm-tree before it cleaves open
rancour, malice, anger, wrath (a characteristic ascribed to the mastigure)

etymology.text: From Proto-Semitic *ṣ́abb-. Compare Classical Syriac ܥܒܐ (ʿabbā, “spiny-tailed lizard”) and Hebrew צַב (ṣaḇ, “turtle”).
